Ships: Top of Page: Vessel boarded was American bark !Gay Head! of San Francisco, J.W. Porter Master, which lwft San Francisco Dec. 28, 1912 hunting whales for oil hauling to Southwasrd then Northwars entering Northern whaling grounds about May 1, 1913. Haring sighted four fur seals about April 1 in about Lat 26N Long 170 W Nat. haring been in Bessing Sea this voyage. haring touched no ports since leaving but expecting to enter some place for potatoes in the near future. 31 mem in crew of whom 9 mem colored, 4 boats, no motor boats, no seal skms nor sealing apparatus. Apparently engaged in business as stated and expecting to leave for San Francisco about Dec 30, 1913

People: 0645: during a fight between H Mitchell, coalheaver and C Howard , Fireman, P Sloane coalheaver without provocation hit Howard on the head with some heavy sharp cornered instrument or monkey wrench causing an ugly scalp wound, which blead profusely.  The surgeon attended the wound and Howard was relived from duty, and placed in sick bay.  After a hearing of the case at the mast the Commanding Officer directed that P. Sloane be confined in the brig with handcuffs on, this was done.
